title: Ficus pallescens
taxonomy: Ficus pallescens is a species of fig tree in the family Moraceae. It is a type of evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Asia. The species Ficus pallescens is classified under the genus Ficus, which includes over 800 species of figs. Ficus pallescens is closely related to other species in the Ficus genus, such as Ficus religiosa and Ficus benghalensis. Further classification of Ficus pallescens is also possible under the subgenus Urostigmataceae.

intro: Ficus pallescens, also known as the pale fig tree, is a species of plant in the family Moraceae, native to tropical regions and valued for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ficus pallescens include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ficus pallescens include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support immune function and overall well-being.
